lenses.guidance: v 47's avodat avodah ('work of service') doubles the Hebrew noun for emphasis. The root avad means both 'work' and 'worship' — Levite hauling is liturgy in disguise, sacred labor and sacred worship sharing a single verb.

vv 15, 18-20's 'they must not touch the holy things or they will die' establishes the shadow of holy distance. 2 Sam 6:6-7's Uzzah — struck dead for steadying the ark — extends the warning. The pattern: holiness kills the unmediated.
